title: Stream Upstream Servers
description: An array of stream upstream servers for dynamic configuration.

items.title: Stream Upstream Server
items.description: Dynamically configurable parameters of a stream upstream server:

id.description: The ID of the stream upstream server. The ID is assigned automatically and cannot be changed.

server.description: Same as the address parameter of the stream upstream server. When adding a server, it is possible to specify it as a domain name. In this case, changes of the IP addresses that correspond to a domain name will be monitored and automatically applied to the upstream configuration without the need of restarting nginx. This requires the resolver directive in the “stream” block. See also the resolve parameter of the stream upstream server.

service.description: Same as the service parameter of the stream upstream server. This parameter cannot be changed.

weight.description: Same as the weight parameter of the stream upstream server.

max_conns.description: Same as the max_conns parameter of the stream upstream server.

max_fails.description: Same as the max_fails parameter of the stream upstream server.

fail_timeout.description: Same as the fail_timeout parameter of the stream upstream server.

slow_start.description: Same as the slow_start parameter of the stream upstream server.

backup.description: When true, adds a backup server. This parameter cannot be changed.

down.description: Same as the down parameter of the stream upstream server.

parent.description: Parent server ID of the resolved server. The ID is assigned automatically and cannot be changed.

host.description: Hostname of the resolved server. The hostname is assigned automatically and cannot be changed.
